blocks.content: After lunch they spread out in the backyard. Eli was on his back in the grass identifying clouds. Savannah was reading — she was always prepared, always had a book somewhere on her. The sky was deep Southern blue with high white clouds that looked permanent but weren't.

Kit sat on the back steps with his knees up. Daisy sat on the top step beside him. Millhaven sounded like this in the afternoon: a lawn mower three streets over, the box fan, a radio too faint to make out the song.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: You were looking at your mom's street up there.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Yeah.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: She doing okay?

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: She's fine. Working Tuesdays and Thursdays now. The diner.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: She like it there?

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Says the people are nice.
He picked at a spot on his knee.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: I think she comes home tired.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: She was tired before, too.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Different tired.
Daisy knew what he meant by that. She didn't push on it.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: My dad used to work doubles at the hardware store. Before he got the supervisor thing. He'd come home and just — not be able to talk for about an hour. Mama called it refilling.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Refilling what.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Himself, I guess. All the parts that talking to people all day uses up.
Kit thought about his mom at the kitchen table. Glass of water, fifteen minutes. Nobody talking.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Yeah. That's the one.
They were quiet for a minute. Eli had found a cloud shaped like a boat and was explaining this to the sky in some detail. Savannah turned a page.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: It's good that she has something. The diner. Even if it's tiring.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: I know.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Better than sitting.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Yeah.
He said it simply, the way he said things when he meant them without wanting to make a whole thing out of it. Daisy let it sit there.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: You want to go to Callaway Park later?
He looked over at her. Something in his face settled — the way it did when something that had been tight in him finally had somewhere to go.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Yeah. That'd be good.
